objective: Fix overlord sweep PentAGI freshness handling so stale or missing reports produce an explicit trigger/skip status and report/dashboard consumers share the same audited repo root/ref.
scope_boundary: Issue #213 planning, execution scope, validation, and closeout evidence
out_of_scope: Running live PentAGI against product repositories during this local implementation session
Changing Codex ingestion, graphify, or memory integrity behavior
research_summary: Issue #213 reports that weekly sweep freshness checks observed missing/stale PentAGI reports in detached audit worktrees but did not trigger or record a concrete skip reason, while dashboard generation read canonical local checkouts. The fix must move PentAGI status into a deterministic helper that accepts an audited repos root and produces one report/dashboard source artifact.

sprints.name: Planning and scope
sprints.goal: Create the issue-backed governance plan, PDCAR, exception, and implementation execution scope before touching sweep code.
acceptance_criteria: Execution scope covers only issue #213 files
No downstream repo writes are required

sprints.name: PentAGI sweep helper
sprints.goal: Make PentAGI freshness and trigger decisions deterministic and testable.
tasks: Add a registry-aware PentAGI sweep helper
Detect fresh, stale, missing-report, missing-token, and missing-runner states
Emit Markdown and JSON from the same audited root/ref
Add regression tests with temporary repos
acceptance_criteria: Repos with security_tier containing pentagi and sweep enabled are evaluated
Unset PENTAGI_API_TOKEN reports SKIPPED: missing PENTAGI_API_TOKEN
Missing runner reports SKIPPED: missing PentAGI runner with expected path
Tests simulate missing/stale PentAGI reports and verify reported trigger/skip status

sprints.name: Sweep and docs wiring
sprints.goal: Use the helper in CI and local sweep instructions so report/dashboard freshness uses the same audited source.
tasks: Wire overlord-sweep workflow to call the helper
Update local overlord-sweep instructions to use the helper and audited repos root
acceptance_criteria: CI report includes PentAGI Trigger Status
Generated metrics/pentagi/latest.json records source root and commit for each repo
Local instructions forbid counting untracked canonical checkout reports for sweep freshness
Dashboard path is documented to consume the same helper output or state when it is not doing so

specialist_reviews.focus: PentAGI helper semantics, workflow wiring, and test coverage
specialist_reviews.summary: Initial review found tracked-only and execute-path gaps; implementation was patched and follow-up review found no blocking findings.

alternate_model_review.summary: No Claude connector is available in this Codex session; same-family implementation is accepted for the narrow governance slice with a focused subagent review and deterministic regression tests required before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Proceed with issue #213 PentAGI sweep source-of-truth fix.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the helper, tests, workflow integration, local sweep instructions, validation evidence, and closeout.

material_deviation_rules: Do not write to downstream product repos.
Do not make PentAGI skips silent.
Do not count untracked canonical checkout reports for detached audit sweep freshness.
If live PentAGI execution is introduced beyond the repo-local runner command, update the plan before implementation.
